title: echinocereus engelmannii subsp. llanuraensis
intro: Echinocereus engelmannii subsp. llanuraensis, also known as a rare cactus species, is a subspecies of the Engelmann prickly pear cactus, which is a type of columnar cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The medicinal properties of Echinocereus engelmannii subsp. llanuraensis include its ability to stimulate the immune system, exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, and have antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and potentially treating various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Echinocereus engelmannii subsp. llanuraensis can be made into salves, creams, or tinctures, which can be applied topically to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
